title: 1. Irithiel VR 372 Landing Page
content: Table of Contents to come. 2. Foreword- VR 372 Players

title: VR- Players
content: Vale Reckoning (VR) is the calendar system used in the Shining Vale during the time of the Revannian Monarchy. VR 1 began at the coronation of King Aeonath the Civilizer, leader of the heroes who pacified the Vale, in the settlement of Haut-Revanne.

title: Other recognized Deities
content: Lady Saoirse The Fates

title: Saints
content: St. Aeonath Patron of Civilization and the Monarchy. Aeonath was the first of the Revannian Kings, and the leader of the adventurers who cleared the Vale for settlement. Aeonath's symbol is a Silver griffon rampant on a white field, or simply a griffon rampant. Aeonath reigned until his death in VR 42, and was sanctified shortly afterwards. The Cathedral to St. Aeonath is in Haut-Revanne. St. Kor Patron of Strength and endurance. A member of Aeonath's group, Kor was a stalwart warrior renowned for his preference to fight without armor, and the effectiveness with which he used his great axe. Kor went off to great adventure after the Vale was settled, but his axe now hangs in the Cathedral to St. Kor in Corwyn City. Kor was sanctified in absentia in VR 20, the first of the Saints. St. Isolde Patron of Art, Progress and Invention. A member of Aeonath's group, Isolde was a clever hero, whose peerless intellect guided the heroes through the most harrowing of troubles. Having started schools, both academic and trade focused, Isolde was canonized in VR 65, despite her great humility protesting the honour. Isolde left the Vale in VR 220, her great skill being needed elsewhere, and is presumed St. Lucia Patron of Healing St. Annabella St. Agatha St. Elyse St. Domingo

title: The Shining Vale- Players
content: "The Shining Vale" refers to a wide valley running in a roughly East-West axis, some 700 miles southeast and inland of the site of fallen Cimmeria. It is so named due to the high volume and quality of gems found in the Barrier Range of mountains which forms most of the natural southern border of the Vale.

title: Piety - Optional Rule
content:
Piety is an Optional Rule from the Mythic Odysseys of Theros sourcebook for 5th Edition Dungeons and Dragons, published in 2020.
Some of the Deities presented in this text are adapted from Deities found in that book, but made more suitable to the world of Irithiel; but most of the Deities have been created herein, drawing of course on the mythology of cultures found in the original and most popular campaign setting, Terra.
